items.title: Feature-engineering encoding decision workflow
items.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  col["categorical column"] --> sem{"nominal or ordinal?"}
  sem -->|"ordinal"| ordLink["ordinal map — see Encoding Categoricals"]
  sem -->|"nominal"| card{"cardinality high?"}
  card -->|"low/medium"| oheLink["one-hot — see Encoding Categoricals"]
  card -->|"high"| alt{"model and memory?"}
  alt -->|"linear / memory tight"| tgtLink["CV target encode — see Encoding Categoricals"]
  alt -->|"trees / no vocab store"| freq["frequency or hash encode"]
  alt -->|"native cat support"| native["pass categorical dtype"]
  oheLink --> pipe["ColumnTransformer in Pipeline"]
  ordLink --> pipe
  tgtLink --> pipe
  freq --> pipe
  native --> pipe
  pipe --> serve["serialize and serve same artifact"]
items.caption: Per-column semantics and cardinality pick the strategy; all paths converge on a fitted pipeline artifact for reproducible serving.

items.title: Train vs serve encoding path
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  train["training split"] --> fit["encoder.fit on train only"]
  fit --> art["saved Pipeline / ColumnTransformer"]
  val["validation / test"] --> transform["encoder.transform"]
  fit --> transform
  prod["production rows"] --> art
  art --> transformProd["transform with frozen mappings"]
  transform --> model["model.predict"]
  transformProd --> model
items.caption: Encoders learn state once from training data; validation, test, and production only transform with the frozen artifact—never refit on new rows.
